mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate directly lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, consumer spending power collapses and recession probability spikes, forcing equity valuations down over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal spending and housing starts signal a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which transmits to corporate revenues and drives negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation, a month-over-month drop in industrial production indicates supply-side contraction that outpaces demand destruction, triggering immediate negative market repricing over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during CPIAUCSL > 210 produce positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2.0% confirm economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in > 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0%, market participants price in imminent recessionary conditions despite liquidity injection, causing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS drops below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During disinflationary periods, rising industrial production signals recovering manufacturing capacity and stabilizing corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during falling CPIAUCSL periods correlate with negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.
